item.piranport.creative_blueprint.toolti: Debug: substitutes for any required blueprint
message.piranport.workbench_in_use: Workbench is in use by another player
message.piranport.use_reload_facility: Use the Reload Facility for torpedo/missile launchers
message.piranport.patchouli_required: Patchouli mod is required to open this book.
piranport.book.name: Piran Port Navigation Manual
piranport.book.landing: A guide to ship rigging, weapons, cooking, aviation, combat buffs, and dungeon sorties. Craft it with a Book and an Aluminum Ingot.
piranport.book.cat.cooking.desc: Crops, processing stations, and naval cuisine.
piranport.book.cat.aviation.desc: Aircraft squadrons, groups, and fire control.
piranport.book.intro.getting_started.p1: Welcome to Piran Port! Follow these steps and you'll be firing guns within half an hour:$(br)$(br)$(bold)1. Mine:$() Find $(item)Bauxite Ore$() on surfaces or in mountains. Breaking it drops $(item)Raw Aluminum$() (Silk Touch drops the ore itself). Smelt to get $(item)Aluminum Ingots$().$(br)$(bold)2. Gather Salt:$() Zombies occasionally drop $(item)Salt$(), or find $(item)Salt Blocks$() in desert/river biomes. Salt can also be made by evaporating water above a lit furnace (see "Salt & Rice").
piranport.book.intro.getting_started.p2: $(bold)3. Craft a Core:$() Use aluminum + salt + wood to craft a $(item)Ship Core$(). Start with $(item)Small Ship Core$() — cheap and agile. You can upgrade later.$(br)$(bold)4. Equip Weapons:$() $(bold)Right-click$() a core to open its GUI. Put weapons like $(item)Small Gun$() in the weapon slots and matching $(item)shells$() in the ammo bay. Click the fuel button at bottom-left to refuel.$(br)$(bold)5. Transform & Fight:$() $(bold)Shift+Right-click$() the core to transform — now you're a kansen! $(bold)Right-click$() to fire, $(bold)V$() to cycle weapons, $(bold)Shift+Right-click$() again to detransform.
piranport.book.intro.getting_started.p3: $(bold)Key Reference:$()$(br)$(item)V$() — Cycle weapon slot / exit recon$(br)$(item)U$() — Open flight group GUI$(br)$(item)P$() — Single-target lock$(br)$(item)O$() — Add to lock list$(br)$(item)I$() — Clear all locks$(br)$(item)H$() — Auto-launch toggle$(br)$(item)Y$() — Toggle battlefield highlight$(br)$(item)9 / 0$() — Wire-guided torpedo steer left/right$(br)$(br)Don't worry if it's a lot — every feature has a detailed entry later. $(bold)Suggested reading order:$() Cannons → Torpedoes → Cooking → Aviation → Combat → Dungeons → Ruins → Arsenal.
piranport.book.intro.overview.p1: Piran Port is a Warship Girls R themed mod. To begin, mine $(item)Bauxite Ore$() for $(item)Aluminum Ingots$() (drops $(item)Raw Aluminum$() without Silk Touch) and collect $(item)Salt$(). Craft a $(item)Ship Core$() — Small, Medium, Large, or Submarine — and place your weapons inside its GUI.
piranport.book.intro.overview.craft: Four ship core types available (Small/Medium/Large/Submarine). Larger cores hold more weapons and have higher load capacity. Each core has a different fuel capacity.
piranport.book.intro.transform.p1: $(bold)Shift + Right-click$() a Ship Core to toggle transformation. Your speed decreases based on total equipment weight. Enhancement slots accept $(item)Armor Plates$() for bonus defense and extra weight.
piranport.book.intro.transform.p2: While transformed, $(bold)right-click$() to fire the active weapon. Press $(bold)V$() to cycle between weapon slots. Open the Ship Core GUI (right-click while not transformed) to manage weapons, ammo, and enhancements.
piranport.book.intro.fuel.p1: Ship Cores require $(item)Fuel$() to maintain transformation. Fuel can be added using: $(item)Coal$() (+1), $(item)Blaze Rod$() (+1), or $(item)Fuel$() item (+1). Place fuel items onto a Ship Core to refuel.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Fuel capacity:$()$(br)Small: 10 / Medium: 20 / Large: 30 / Submarine: 10$(br)$(br)Fuel is consumed by distance while transformed. When fuel runs out, transformation ends automatically. Aircraft tooltips show $(item)Fueled$() or $(item)Not Fueled$() status.
piranport.book.intro.skins.p1: $(item)Skin Cores$() change your character's appearance. $(bold)Shift+Right-click$() a Skin Core to equip the skin. $(bold)Shift+Right-click$() the same skin again to remove it (core is not consumed). Switching to a different skin returns the old core to your inventory and consumes the new one.$(br)$(br)Three Skin Cores are available (I/II/III), each with a unique appearance. Skin state is synced across all players.
piranport.book.cannons.basics.p1: Four gun types: $(item)Single Small Gun$() (1 barrel), $(item)Small Gun$() (twin 2 barrels), $(item)Medium Gun$() (1 barrel), $(item)Large Gun$() (triple 3 barrels). Barrel count determines ammo consumed per salvo.$(br)$(br)$(item)HE Shells$() cause area explosions. $(item)AP Shells$() deal higher armor-piercing damage.
piranport.book.cannons.basics.craft: Shells must match gun caliber. Unfilled medium/large guns show an empty durability bar as a reminder.
piranport.book.cannons.advanced.p1: $(bold)VT Shell (Proximity Fuze):$() Small caliber only. Follows the same trajectory as HE shells but detonates automatically near the target. Effective against fast-moving targets.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Type 3 Shell (Sanshiki):$() Available in Small/Medium/Large caliber. Fires a spread of 64 pellets in a cone pattern. Each pellet deals 1/4 of the equivalent HE shell's damage. Effective against dense target groups or aircraft squadrons.
piranport.book.torpedoes.basics.p1: Torpedo launchers (Twin/Triple/Quad) fire 533mm or 610mm torpedoes in a spread pattern. Torpedoes travel across water surfaces and inflict the $(item)Flooding$() debuff on hit. They self-destruct after 1 minute if they miss.
piranport.book.torpedoes.basics.craft: Twin launcher fires 2 torpedoes in a ±3° spread. Larger launchers spread wider.
piranport.book.cooking.crops.p1: Eight crops can be grown: $(item)Tomato$(), $(item)Soybean$(), $(item)Chili$(), $(item)Onion$(), $(item)Rice$(), $(item)Lettuce$(), $(item)Garlic$(), and $(item)Pineapple$(). Seeds drop from tall grass or wandering traders. Plant on farmland; Rice grows in waterlogged soil.
piranport.book.cooking.stations.p1: $(bold)Stone Mill:$() Right-click to open. Grind crops into flour, powder, and paste. Accepts up to 4 ingredients and produces 1-2 outputs.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Cutting Board:$() Right-click to place an ingredient, then right-click repeatedly to cut. Shift+right-click to retrieve.
piranport.book.cooking.stations.p2: $(bold)Cooking Pot:$() Place over a heat source (fire, campfire, magma, lava). Right-click to open GUI. Add ingredients; the pot cooks automatically when heated. Recipes combine 1-9 inputs into a single food output.
piranport.book.aviation.aircraft.p1: Six aircraft types: $(item)Fighter$() (hovers and fires bullets, 64 rounds), $(item)Dive Bomber$() (dives close and drops a bomb), $(item)Torpedo Bomber$() (fires torpedo at 20-30 blocks range), $(item)Level Bomber$() (drops bombs from 32 blocks above, 8 rounds), $(item)ASW Aircraft$() (drops depth charges, targets only submarines/aquatic mobs), and $(item)Recon Squadron$() (see Recon entry).
piranport.book.aviation.aircraft.p2: Press $(bold)[U]$() while transformed to open Groups directly. Assign aircraft slots (left-click), right-click to cycle ammo: $(item)Fighter Ammo$() for fighters, $(item)Aerial Bomb$() for bombers, $(item)Aerial Torpedo$() for torpedo bombers. No ammo assigned = cannot launch.
piranport.book.aviation.fire_control.p1: While transformed: $(bold)[P]$() locks crosshair target (80-block range); $(bold)[O]$() adds target; $(bold)[I]$() cancels all locks. Locked targets shown in top-right HUD. Press $(bold)[Y]$() to toggle battlefield highlight — your aircraft/torpedoes/bombs glow. Only visible to you.
piranport.book.aviation.recon.p1: The $(item)Recon Squadron$() is a non-combat aircraft used for scouting. It carries no weapons or ammo. After launching, press $(bold)[V]$() to enter recon view — your camera follows the aircraft in real time. Press $(bold)[V]$() again to exit. The recon plane force-loads chunks around it so you can see distant terrain. Useful for spotting enemy positions before committing to an attack.
piranport.book.cat.combat.desc: Combat effects, buff foods, and battlefield tactics.
piranport.book.combat.effects.p1: $(bold)Flooding:$() Applied by torpedo hits. Slows and damages the target over time.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Reload Boost:$() Increases ammo resupply speed. Levels I-III available. Obtained from food like $(item)Chicken Tatsuta$() or $(item)Torpedo Juice$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Evasion:$() Grants a dodge chance (15-35% based on level) while transformed. Obtained from $(item)Tempura$() or $(item)Kvass$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Flammable:$() Fire hazard debuff when loading aviation fuel (disabled by default in config).
piranport.book.combat.buff_foods.p1: $(item)Chicken Tatsuta$() — $(bold)Reload Boost I$() for 180s.$(br)$(br)$(item)Torpedo Juice$() — $(bold)Hunger II + Reload Boost II + Fire Resistance I$(); returns bottle.$(br)$(br)$(item)Tempura$() — $(bold)Evasion I$() for 180s.$(br)$(br)$(item)Kvass$() — $(bold)Slowness I + Slow Falling I + Evasion II$() for 120s; returns bottle.$(br)$(br)Cook these at the $(item)Cooking Pot$() using standard ingredients.
piranport.book.cooking.world_dishes.p1: The mod includes a rich variety of dishes from around the world, all crafted at the $(item)Cooking Pot$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Chinese:$() $(item)Dongpo Pork$(), $(item)Deluxe Baozi$(), $(item)Tangyuan$(), $(item)Assorted Char Siu Fried Rice$()$(br)$(bold)Japanese:$() $(item)Soba Noodle$(), $(item)Tempura Soba$(), $(item)Yokan$()$(br)$(bold)German:$() $(item)Schweinshaxe$(), $(item)Thuringer Rostbratwurst$(), $(item)Thuringer Rostbratwurst und Bier$(), $(item)Weisswurst mit Bagel$(), $(item)Black Forest Gateau$()
piranport.book.cooking.world_dishes.p2: $(bold)Russian:$() $(item)Borscht$(), $(item)Okroshka$(), $(item)Russian Dumpling$(), $(item)Rye Bread$(), $(item)New Rye Bread$()$(br)$(bold)British:$() $(item)Yorkshire Pudding$(), $(item)Eggs Benedict$(), $(item)Black Tea Scone$(), $(item)Black Tea Sandwich$(), $(item)Royal Naval Salted Beef$()$(br)$(bold)Others:$() $(item)Bouillabaisse$(), $(item)Salami Pizza$(), $(item)Venice Cuttlefish Noodles$(), $(item)Macaron$(), $(item)Tarte Tatin$(), $(item)Double/Triple Shell Burger$(), $(item)Barbecue$(), etc.$(br)$(br)All dishes can be placed on the ground for shared eating.
piranport.book.cooking.salt_rice.p1: $(bold)Salt Evaporation:$() Place a $(item)Water Source$() block directly above a lit $(item)Furnace$(). After 10 seconds, the water converts to a $(item)Salt Block$(). Flowing water above a furnace produces $(item)Salt Chips$() instead.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Rice Paddies:$() $(item)Rice$() must be planted on waterlogged farmland — place water and plant rice seeds on the flooded block. If the water is removed, the crop breaks.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Salt Ore:$() Can be enabled in config to generate naturally in river biomes.
piranport.book.cat.dungeon.desc: Sea area sorties and node-based combat.
piranport.book.dungeon.basics.p1: Obtain a $(item)Dungeon Key$() and find a $(item)Dungeon Lectern$(). Right-click the lectern with a key to open the sortie screen. Choose a chapter and stage, then press $(bold)Sortie$() to enter. Each stage has multiple nodes connected by $(item)Portals$(). Walk into a portal to advance to the next node.
piranport.book.dungeon.basics.p2: At each node, defeat all enemies to proceed. Collect supplies from $(item)Loot Ships$() along the way. If you fall in combat, you may revive by consuming a $(item)Totem of Undying$(), or give up and return to the lobby. Use a $(item)Town Scroll$() to exit the dungeon early. First clear of each stage grants bonus rewards. Your progress is saved on the $(item)Dungeon Key$().
message.piranport.no_fuel: No fuel! Use a fuel bucket to refuel
item.piranport.town_scroll.recon_blocked: Cannot use Town Scroll in recon mode
block.piranport.dungeon_lectern.no_key: You need a Dungeon Key to use this lectern

gui.piranport.dungeon_revive.cost: Consume 1 Totem of Undying to return?
gui.piranport.dungeon_result.title: Result
gui.piranport.town_scroll.title: Return
gui.piranport.town_scroll.question: Leave dungeon?
dungeon.piranport.stage_locked: Cannot change stage: a dungeon run is in progress on this key.
dungeon.piranport.revive_unavailable: Cannot revive: no active dungeon found.
dungeon.piranport.flagship_promoted: You are now the flagship; the dungeon key has been transferred to your copy.
dungeon.piranport.artillery_intro.incomi: Transport plane incoming — supplies on the way!
dungeon.piranport.artillery_intro.crate_: Supply crate has landed — collect your weapons!
message.piranport.torpedo_guidance_enter: Wire-Guided Torpedo View [V to Cut Wire]
tooltip.piranport.eugen_shield.block: Blocks damage from a 150° frontal arc
message.piranport.ship_girl_greet: Hello, Admiral! How can I help?
tooltip.piranport.chaos_shard: A shard of chaotic will, portal activation material
tooltip.piranport.flag: A national flag, skin crafting material
tooltip.piranport.exp_shell: Can be used to enhance equipment
piranport.book.cat.ruins.desc: Four types of ruin structures scattered across the ocean: Portal Ruins, Supply Depots, Outposts, and Abyssal Bases.
piranport.book.ruins.overview.p1: Across the ocean biomes, you will find four types of ruin structures.$(br)$(br)§lPortal Ruins§r — Ancient portal frames on the ocean floor. No enemies, but frame materials can be collected.$(br)§lSupply Depots§r — Small building remnants with light enemy guards and basic loot.
piranport.book.ruins.overview.p2: §lOutposts§r — Medium military facilities with runway structures, more abyssal NPCs, and richer loot.$(br)§lAbyssal Bases§r — Large bases found only in deep ocean biomes. Multiple runways, many high-level enemies, and the best loot and rare materials.$(br)$(br)Make sure to equip your rigging before exploring!
piranport.book.ruins.enemies.p1: The abyssal forces consist of 9 ship types:$(br)§lSupply§r — Harmless supply unit$(br)§lDestroyer§r — Fast light cannons$(br)§lLight Cruiser§r — Cannons + torpedoes$(br)§lHeavy Cruiser§r — Heavy guns + high armor$(br)§lBattle Cruiser§r — High-damage tracking shells
piranport.book.ruins.enemies.p2: §lBattleship§r — Strongest cannons + highest defense$(br)§lLight Carrier§r — Launches a few aircraft$(br)§lCarrier§r — Large aircraft squadrons$(br)§lSubmarine§r — Underwater stealth + torpedo attacks$(br)$(br)Abyssal NPCs have fleet alert AI — attacking one may alert the entire fleet!
piranport.book.ruins.loot.p1: Each ruin type contains different tiers of loot:$(br)$(br)§lPortal Ruins§r — Basic materials, some portal frame blocks$(br)§lSupply Depots§r — Small equipment, resources, flags, sortie channel (rare)$(br)§lOutposts§r — Medium/large equipment, aircraft, exp shells, chaos shards (rare)
piranport.book.ruins.loot.p2: §lAbyssal Bases§r — All tiers of equipment, abyssal reports, chaos shards, portal activation cores (rare)$(br)$(br)Chaos shards come in 9 varieties (α-ι), key materials for activating the deep sea portal in a future update. Flags can be used in skin crafting.
block.piranport.confidential_cargo.toolt: Too naive. It's classified cargo — of course there's no description.
block.piranport.italian_dish_kit.tooltip: An essential companion for enjoying Mediterranean sun and sea breeze.
piranport.book.cat.arsenal.desc: Missiles, depth charges, special equipment, and weapon crafting facilities.
piranport.book.arsenal.missiles.p1: Missiles come in three types:$(br)$(br)$(bold)Anti-Ship Missiles:$() $(item)SY-1 Launcher$() (30 damage +6 AP, burst 2) and $(item)MK14 Harpoon Launcher$() (24 damage, burst 4). Right-click to launch; auto-tracks nearest target ahead (excludes underwater targets and containers), or tracks fire-control locked target.
piranport.book.arsenal.missiles.p2: $(bold)Anti-Air Missiles:$() $(item)Terrier Launcher$(), $(item)Sea Dart Launcher$(), $(item)Seacat Launcher$(). With $(bold)[H]$() auto-launch enabled, they auto-fire when airborne enemies are within 32 blocks, prioritizing fire-control locked targets.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Rockets:$() $(item)Ship Rocket Launcher$(), fires straight with no guidance.$(br)$(br)Unfilled launchers show an empty durability bar as a reminder.
piranport.book.arsenal.depth_charges.p1: $(item)Depth Charge Launchers$() deploy $(item)Depth Charges$() against underwater targets. Three models:$(br)$(br)$(bold)Depth Charge Launcher$() — 1 charge, single drop$(br)$(bold)DC Launcher Improved$() — 2 charges, fore-aft spread$(br)$(bold)DC Launcher Advanced$() — 3 charges, triangle spread$(br)$(br)Depth charges sink automatically and detonate via $(bold)proximity fuze$() when near a target, dealing area magic damage.
piranport.book.arsenal.special.p1: $(bold)Combat Items:$(br)$(item)Damage Control$() — Clears all debuffs and extinguishes fire$(br)$(item)Repair Kit$() — Hold right-click on a mob to continuously heal$(br)$(item)Smoke Candle$() — Deploys a smoke screen block$(br)$(item)Flare Launcher$() — Fires a flare that creates light on blocks, makes mobs glow on hit
piranport.book.arsenal.special.p2: $(bold)Special Weapons:$(br)$(item)Mysterious Weapon$() (Railgun) — Gravity-ignoring high-speed projectile$(br)$(item)Gungnir$() — Throwing weapon$(br)$(item)Eugen's Ship Shield$() — Blocks 150° frontal arc$(br)$(item)Hatsuyuki's Main Gun$() — Axe-type weapon$(br)$(br)$(bold)Equipment:$(br)$(item)Kirin-class Headband$() — Permanent invisibility$(br)$(item)Football Superstar Set$() (4-piece) — Experience Boost buff
piranport.book.arsenal.crafting.p1: $(bold)Weapon Workbench:$() Right-click to open GUI. 5 tabs (Cannons/Torpedo Launchers/Missile Launchers/Depth Charges/Aircraft). Select a recipe, insert materials, and click Craft. Some advanced weapons require $(item)Blueprints$(). Crafting takes time; collect the result when done.
piranport.book.arsenal.crafting.p2: $(bold)Ammo Workbench:$() Craft various ammo types (shells, torpedoes, missiles, depth charges).$(br)$(br)$(bold)Reload Facility:$() Insert an empty launcher and matching ammo to load it. Works with cannons, torpedo launchers, and missile launchers.
piranport.book.aviation.asw.p1: $(item)ASW Aircraft$() specialize in attacking underwater targets. Currently available: $(item)Swordfish Mk3$() and $(item)TBF (ASW)$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Attack behavior:$() Only attacks submarines and aquatic mobs. Flies over the target and drops $(item)Depth Charges$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Sonar detection:$() ASW aircraft scan for underwater entities every second. Detected targets are highlighted with a $(bold)yellow outline$(). Sonar highlighting is independent of fire-control and Y-key highlights.
piranport.book.torpedoes.named.p1: Multiple torpedo variants exist, each with unique damage, range, and speed.$(br)$(br)$(bold)533mm Series:$() Standard, Magnetic (magnetic fuze), Acoustic (homing), Wire-Guided (press $(bold)[9]/[0]$() to steer manually).$(br)$(br)$(bold)German G7 Series:$() G7a standard/magnetic, G7e electric magnetic/acoustic/wire-guided.
piranport.book.torpedoes.named.p2: $(bold)US MK Series:$() MK14 standard, MK16/MK17 hydrogen peroxide, MK27 acoustic.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Japanese Series:$() Type 91 610mm, Type 93 Mk1 high-speed / Mk3 long-range (oxygen torpedoes), Type 95 530mm/610mm submarine torpedoes, Type 0 720mm heavy torpedo.$(br)$(br)Hover over a torpedo item to view its specific damage/range/speed stats.
